title: 1981-04-16_Pat_24.txt
key_claims: Dick formulates a vital amendment to Descartes' *Cogito, ergo sum*: "I know I believe [in the world], and I know I cannot compel my own belief. Therefore, an agency beyond myself with the power to compel belief exists."

For human functionality, the metaphysical existence of the universe is irrelevant; what matters pragmatically is that *the universe seems real to us*. God, acting as Cosmogenator, enforces this seeming.

Dick sharply distinguishes his view from Spinoza's pantheism: God is *not* the reality field. God is the transcendent agency that ceaselessly perturbs the reality field by introducing ex nihilo newness.

God revealed the binary switching model to Dick because it was the specific "Gestalt" (information-processing mechanism) that Dick's modern mind could comprehend and assent to, thus inferring God from the mechanism.
recurring_concepts: **Pretextual Cause**: Re-emphasized as the miracle of creation occurring inside normal mechanical causality—the "effect" exceeding its putative deterministic "cause" because God has covertly injected new telemetry.
reading_excerpt: Dick formulates a vital amendment to Descartes' *Cogito, ergo sum*: "I know I believe [in the world], and I know I cannot compel my own belief. Therefore, an agency beyond myself with the power to compel belief exists."
